title: Marbella Luxury Yacht Charter Weekend: A Complete 2026 Guide
html:
Planning a luxury yacht charter weekend in Marbella typically starts from Puerto Banús, where a crewed 50-foot motor yacht costs around €6,000 for two days. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners offering everything from sleek sports cruisers to spacious catamarans, ensuring a seamless Mediterranean escape.

How much does a luxury yacht charter weekend in Marbella cost?

A weekend crewed charter starts at €5,000 for a 40-foot yacht, rising to €15,000 for a 70-foot superyacht. This includes skipper, fuel, and standard amenities. For a breakdown, a half-day on a small motorboat costs from €400, while a full-day catamaran for ten people averages €2,500. You can compare prices and availability for a yacht charter Marbella weekend on the platform.

High season (June to August) commands a 30, 50% premium over shoulder months. Booking through BoatHire24 ensures transparent pricing and no hidden fees.

Where to set off in Marbella?

Depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina lined with restaurants and designer boutiques. Alternatively, Marbella’s Real Club de Mar offers a quieter base with direct access to the Mediterranean. For a more local vibe, try the fishing port of La Bajadilla, or head east to Cabopino Marina near the Artola sand dunes. Overnight trips often anchor at Isla de Alborán or the charming cove of Cala de Marbella.

Which boat to rent for a weekend?

  1. Azimut 50 Fly, ideal for a couple seeking luxury and a flybridge for sundowners, sleeps 6 guests, from €6,000 per weekend.
  2. Sunseeker 68 Sport, perfect for a group of 8 who want speed and style, with a top speed of 36 knots, from €12,000 per weekend.
  3. Lagoon 46 catamaran, best for families or two couples, offering stability and space for 8 guests, from €7,500 per weekend.
  4. Fairline Squadron 58, a balanced choice for 6 guests, combining comfort and performance, from €8,000 per weekend. For specifications on a similar model, check the Princess V58 listing on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a yacht in Marbella?

Skippered charters require no licence; bareboat rentals need a valid ICC or equivalent. For crewed charters, the owner provides a professional skipper, so you simply relax. If you prefer to drive yourself, the operator on BoatHire24 will confirm the exact licence requirements for your chosen boat. In 2026, the Spanish authorities accept the ICC for most vessels under 24 metres.

For a weekend, book a skipper to enjoy the Costa del Sol’s famous poniente wind, which cools the afternoons from June to September. The skipper knows the best anchorages, like the secluded Cala de la Torrecilla.

At a glance

  • Price range: €5,000, €15,000 per weekend (crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: June to August
  • Trip length: 2 days (48 hours)
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a luxury yacht for a weekend in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, crewed charters include a professional skipper, so no licence is needed; you can simply enjoy the experience.

What is the best time of year for a weekend charter in Marbella?

May, June, and September offer warm weather, fewer crowds, and lower prices than July and August.

How far is Puerto Banús from the Marbella town centre?

Puerto Banús is approximately 6 kilometres southwest of Marbella town, a 10-minute taxi ride along the coast.

Can I include water toys on my charter?

Most crewed yachts offer optional jetskis, paddleboards, and snorkelling gear for an additional fee.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. Whether you are planning a weekend escape or a longer voyage, the platform connects you with verified boats and real owners. Owners can list your boat on BoatHire24 to reach a global audience.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
